caption: Entity Management
description:
Entity Management events report activity by a managed client, a micro service, or a user at a management console. The activity can be a create, read, update, and delete operation on a managed entity.

When to use this class: Use for CRUD operations on non-user entities such as devices, organizations, policies, email objects, or network zones — anything managed via the managed_entity object. Also use as a catch-all when no more specific IAM class applies. Do not use for user account lifecycle changes (use Account Change), user privilege assignments (use User Access Management), or group membership changes (use Group Management). While those are technically entity management, the specialized classes provide richer attributes for those specific use cases.
name: entity_management

5.caption: Move
5.description: Move or rename an existing managed entity.

8.caption: Enable
8.description: Enable an existing managed entity. Note: This is typically regarded as a semi-permanent, editor visible, syncable change.

9.caption: Disable
9.description: Disable an existing managed entity. Note: This is typically regarded as a semi-permanent, editor visible, syncable change.

10.caption: Activate
10.description: Activate an existing managed entity. Note: This is a typically regarded as a transient change, a change of state of the engine.

11.caption: Deactivate
11.description: Deactivate an existing managed entity. Note: This is a typically regarded as a transient change, a change of state of the engine.

comment.description: The user provided comment about why the entity was changed.
